report_name: Conti Ransomware Coder Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court for Wire Fraud Conspiracy

threat_actors.name: Conti
threat_actors.description: A prolific Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group responsible for over $150 million in ransom payments from over 1,000 victims worldwide. The group was known for double extortion tactics and publicly supported Russia's invasion of Ukraine before dissolving in 2022.

threat_actors.name: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko
threat_actors.description: A 44-year-old Ukrainian national and malware developer for the Conti ransomware gang. He pleaded guilty to creating a malware loader used to deploy the Conti ransomware payload.

threat_actors.name: Black Basta
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group believed to be a successor to the Conti gang after its dissolution.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: A malware loader developed by Oleksii Lytvynenko was used to download and transfer the main Conti ransomware tool into the victim's network from a command-and-control server.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Conti ransomware payload, delivered by the loader, encrypted victim files as the primary method of impact and extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The article notes that loaders often use scripting to execute payloads, and a pre-extracted observable points to the use of encoded PowerShell commands, a common loader technique.

attack_patterns.name: Regsvr32
attack_patterns.description: The pre-extracted observables indicate the use of regsvr32.exe, a LOLBIN often abused by malware to execute malicious scripts or DLLs, likely as part of the loader's execution chain.

malware.name: Conti
malware.description: The main ransomware payload deployed by the Conti group, delivered by a loader developed by Lytvynenko. It was used to encrypt victim files and extort over $150 million.

malware.name: Conti Loader
malware.description: A malware loader developed by Oleksii Lytvynenko. Its primary function was to download and execute the main Conti ransomware payload from a C2 server onto a victim's system.

malware.name: Black Basta
malware.description: Ransomware used by a successor group to Conti.

malware.name: Quantum
malware.description: Ransomware associated with a successor group to Conti.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure Victims
identities.description: Conti targeted over 1,000 victims worldwide, including critical infrastructure sectors like hospitals and schools.

infrastructure.name: Conti C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command-and-control server used by the Conti loader to download the main ransomware executable.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: 2b9a8b3c-1f7e-4d9a-8c6b-0e1d2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of PowerShell with the '-enc' argument, which is frequently used by malware loaders to execute obfuscated commands.
references:
  - https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/ukrainian-national-pleads-guilty-wire-fraud-conspiracy-connection-conti-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -enc '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of encoded PowerShell commands, a technique often used by malware loaders to obscure their actions, as mentioned in the pre-extracted observables.

indicators.name: Executable Download from Uncategorized Domain
indicators.pattern:
title: Executable Download from Uncategorized Domain
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-8b9a-1e2d3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects HTTP GET requests for executable files (.exe, .dll) from domains that are not categorized as corporate or well-known software distributors. This is a common behavior for malware loaders.
references:
  - https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/ukrainian-national-pleads-guilty-wire-fraud-conspiracy-connection-conti-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|endswith:
      - '.exe'
      - '.dll'
    cs-method: 'GET'
  filter_known_good:
    r-dns|endswith:
      - '.microsoft.com'
      - '.windowsupdate.com'
      - '.google.com'
      - '.apple.com'
      - '.adobe.com'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: Detects network traffic patterns consistent with a malware loader downloading a secondary payload, such as an HTTP GET request for an executable from an unknown domain.

indicators.name: Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution
id: 5a4b3c2d-1f0e-4d9a-8c7b-6e5d4f3a2b1c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of regsvr32.exe, a LOLBIN often abused by malware to execute malicious scripts or DLLs.
references:
  - https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/ukrainian-national-pleads-guilty-wire-fraud-conspiracy-connection-conti-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
tags:
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of regsvr32.exe, a known LOLBIN that can be used to proxy execution of malicious code, based on pre-extracted cyber observables.
